text.title: Debt Payoff Strategies
text.summary: Four execution strategies for paying down debt — Boy Scout Rule, feature-attached, dedicated sprint, Strangler Fig — with selection criteria per item profile.

rule.statement: For each debt item, choose the payoff strategy based on its profile: Boy Scout for low-cost incidental items (same file, <= 20 LOC change), feature-attached for items in code about to change for a feature, dedicated sprint for high-impact isolated items, Strangler Fig for high-cost systemic replacement.
rule.rationale: Applying the wrong strategy wastes effort: Strangler Fig for a 10-line function is over-engineering; Boy Scout for a 500-line God class is scope explosion. Matching strategy to profile keeps PRs focused and reviewable.

section.title: Boy Scout Rule
section.p: Leave code better than you found it — make 1-3 small improvements while working on related code in the same file. Cap at 20 LOC per cleanup; reject same-PR scope expansion to other files.

example.note: Type hints, docstring, comprehension — all under 20 LOC delta, no API change, no new tests required.

section.title: Feature-Attached Payoff
section.p: When planning a feature that touches debt-ridden code: identify debt in the affected area, include payoff in the feature estimate, pay debt before building the feature. Prevents adding new code on top of existing debt.

example.code: Feature: Add subscription tiers Affected code: User billing module (has TD-003 — missing type hints, mixed concerns) Approach: Fix TD-003 first, then build feature on clean foundation Benefit: Feature code is cleaner; no debt-on-debt layering

section.title: Strangler Fig Pattern
section.p: Gradually replace legacy system by routing traffic to a modern facade. Use only when the legacy interface is stable enough to wrap. If the interface is also debt, rewrite atomically inside a feature flag instead.

example.note: Start routing a subset of traffic (standard/premium orders). Expand flag coverage as confidence grows. Remove legacy path only after 100% rollout and monitoring.

rule.statement: Do not let agents ship the Strangler Fig modern path to more than 1% of traffic without human sign-off and a rollback feature flag.
rule.rationale: Agents underestimate migration cost by 2-3x. The Strangler Fig involves production routing changes; early errors affect real users.
